skill.name: probast-quality-assessment-for-prediction-model-studies
skill.description: Assess risk of bias in medical prediction model studies using the PROBAST framework. Evaluates four domains: Participants, Predictors, Outcome, and Analysis. Supports PDF text extraction and produces structured JSON reports.
skill.summary: Apply the PROBAST framework to assess risk of bias in medical prediction model studies across four domains with structured JSON output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 59 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives with no confirmed security threats. The 19 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched substrings like 'DES' in argparse 'RawDescriptionHelpFormatter' and 'description' YAML keys, not actual cryptographic code. The 37 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections matched markdown code fence delimiters, not shell operators. The remaining filesystem, network, and reconnaissance detections correspond to legitimate PDF text extraction output, source repository attribution, and workflow documentation. The skill is a legitimate medical research quality assessment tool with bounded and transparent functionality. No malicious intent indicators were found.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command References in Documentation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains documentation of shell commands for running the PDF extraction script (e.g., 'python scripts/extract_pdf.py --help'). These are instructional references for the AI agent to execute, not a security threat. The commands are bounded and do not accept arbitrary user input for injection.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations
medium_findings.description: scripts/extract_pdf.py writes extracted text content to an output file. This is expected behavior for a PDF text extraction tool and the output path is controlled through the --output parameter. No arbitrary file write capability exists beyond the intended extraction workflow.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer identified 37 instances of markdown triple-backtick code fences as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are standard markdown code block delimiters in the prompt template references and do not execute commands. All instances are false positives.

low_findings.title: Source Repository URL Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL linking to the GitHub source repository. This is attribution documentation, not a network call. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network communication is possible through this static reference.

content.user_title: Assess Bias in Medical Prediction Models with PROBAST
content.value_statement: Researchers need to evaluate the quality and risk of bias in medical prediction model studies. This skill applies the validated PROBAST framework to assess bias across four domains and generates structured JSON output for reproducible systematic reviews.
actual_capabilities: Extract metadata (first author and publication year) from medical research papers
Assess risk of bias across four PROBAST domains: Participants, Predictors, Outcome, and Analysis
Determine overall risk of bias using structured decision rules from the PROBAST framework
Extract text content from PDF files for full-text analysis using the built-in extraction script
Generate structured JSON output with domain-level ratings and overall bias assessment
Follow a reproducible multi-step workflow with documented prompts for each assessment stage
limitations: Does not perform statistical analysis or compute model performance metrics from study data
Assessment quality depends on completeness and transparency of the source paper
Cannot evaluate prediction models without access to the full paper text or sufficient extract
Does not replace domain expertise for nuanced methodological judgment beyond the rubric

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Quality Assessment
use_cases.description: Evaluate multiple prediction model studies during a systematic review using a consistent and reproducible PROBAST framework across all papers.

use_cases.title: Peer Review Support
use_cases.description: Assess the methodological quality of submitted manuscripts that develop or validate clinical prediction models as part of the peer review process.

use_cases.title: Research Methodology Training
use_cases.description: Learn how to apply the PROBAST tool by practicing structured bias assessment on published studies with guided prompts and decision rules.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Bias Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the risk of bias of this medical prediction model study using the PROBAST framework. Focus on whether appropriate data sources were used and whether predictors were defined consistently across participants.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides paper text and wants a fast preliminary bias assessment

prompt_templates.title: Full PROBAST Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete PROBAST risk of bias assessment on the following paper. Evaluate all four domains: Participants, Predictors, Outcome, and Analysis. Provide a rating for each domain and an overall bias rating with reasoning.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides paper text and wants a thorough evaluation with full documentation

prompt_templates.title: PDF-Based Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from the PDF file at the specified path using the PDF extraction script, then perform a full PROBAST risk of bias assessment on the extracted text. Output the results as structured JSON.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a PDF file path instead of plain text

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Bias Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess and compare the risk of bias across these three prediction model studies using PROBAST. For each study provide domain-level ratings and an overall risk rating. Highlight the methodology differences that explain divergent ratings.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to compare multiple studies side by side

output_examples.input: Assess bias in the paper by Wang et al. 2018 about a prediction model for cardiovascular disease using PROBAST.
output_examples.output: Domain 1 (Participants) rated Low risk. The study used an appropriate cohort data source with clear inclusion criteria. Domain 2 (Predictors) rated Low risk. Predictors were measured consistently and blinded to outcome. Domain 3 (Outcome) rated Low risk. Standard outcome definitions were used with consistent determination. Domain 4 (Analysis) rated High risk. Complete-case analysis was used for 30 percent missing data without sensitivity analysis, and events-per-variable was below 10. Overall bias rating: High risk.

output_examples.input: Compare PROBAST assessments for the three prediction model papers in my review folder about postoperative complication prediction.
output_examples.output: Paper A (Smith 2020): Low risk in all four domains with strong methodological reporting. Paper B (Jones 2021): High risk in Analysis domain due to low events-per-variable ratio of 8 and no internal validation. Paper C (Lee 2022): Unclear risk in Outcome domain because the outcome definition and determination process were not fully described. A summary comparison table has been generated alongside the detailed JSON output for each study.

best_practices: Always read the full paper text before assigning risk ratings to avoid incomplete or inaccurate assessments.
Document the rationale for each risk rating explicitly to ensure reproducibility and transparency in systematic reviews.
Assess each domain independently using the parallel workflow to prevent cross-domain influence on ratings.
anti_patterns: Do not skip domains or assign ratings without reviewing the corresponding PROBAST signaling questions for that domain.
Do not modify the structured JSON output format, as consistency is required for aggregation across multiple assessments in systematic reviews.
Do not assess papers based on abstracts or summaries alone. Full text is required for an accurate and complete PROBAST evaluation.

faq.question: What is PROBAST?
faq.answer: PROBAST is a validated tool for assessing risk of bias in prediction model studies. It evaluates four domains: Participants, Predictors, Outcome, and Analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze a PDF file?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a script to extract text from PDF files before performing the bias assessment, enabling full-text analysis of papers in PDF format.

faq.question: What output format does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a structured JSON report with risk ratings for each of the four domains and an overall bias rating, following a predefined schema.

faq.question: Does this skill replace expert methodological review?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides a structured framework for assessment, but domain expertise is still required for nuanced methodological judgment beyond the rubric.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-medical prediction models?
faq.answer: No. PROBAST is specifically designed for medical prediction model studies and may not be appropriate for prediction models in other domains.

faq.question: What are the four PROBAST domains?
faq.answer: The four domains are: Participants (selection and data sources), Predictors (definition and measurement), Outcome (determination and classification), and Analysis (statistical methods and reporting).
